checklist_item.title: Does the P2P peer actor correctly validate incoming ENR eth2 fields for BPO fork compatibility?
checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Locate the ENR validation logic in the P2P peer handling code.
2. Verify that BPO fork detection correctly identifies when a fork is BPO vs. regular.
3. Test that peers with correctly computed BPO fork ENR eth2 fields are accepted.
4. Test that peers with incorrectly computed ENR eth2 fields (next_fork_version != current_fork_version for BPO) are handled appropriately.
5. Verify the validation logic does not inadvertently reject compatible peers at BPO fork boundaries.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-51, Supporting Node: actor-p2p-peer, Source Invariant: p2p:SG-012:INV-002

checklist_item.title: Does the beacon node correctly compute and advertise ENR eth2 field for BPO forks?
checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Locate the beacon node's ENR eth2 field computation logic.
2. Identify how the beacon node determines whether an upcoming fork is BPO or regular.
3. Verify the beacon node sets next_fork_version = current_fork_version for BPO forks.
4. Verify the beacon node sets next_fork_epoch to the planned BPO epoch.
5. Test beacon node ENR advertisement at various epochs relative to a BPO fork activation.
6. Compare ENR output between different client implementations to ensure consistency.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-51, Supporting Node: actor-beacon-node, Source Invariant: p2p:SG-012:INV-002

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for tb-009: Is the ENR eth2 field correctly signaling no upcoming fork by setting next_fork_version == current_fork_version AND next_fork_epoch == FAR_FUTURE_EPOCH?
checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Identify the ENR eth2 field computation logic in the beacon node codebase (p2p:SG-012:enr_eth2_field_computation).
2. Review the code path that determines when no fork is planned and how the ENR fields are set.
3. Verify that when no fork is scheduled, next_fork_version is set equal to current_fork_version.
4. Verify that when no fork is scheduled, next_fork_epoch is set to FAR_FUTURE_EPOCH (0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFF or equivalent).
5. Test by simulating a state with no planned fork and inspecting the advertised ENR eth2 field values.
6. Verify that peers correctly interpret the no-fork signal and do not reject the node as being on a different fork schedule.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-52, Trust Boundary: tb-009, Invariant: p2p:SG-012:INV-003, Threat: stride-017

checklist_item.title: Does the beacon node (actor-beacon-node) correctly compute ENR eth2 fields for peer discovery when transitioning between fork states?
checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Locate the beacon node's ENR update logic that handles fork schedule changes.
2. Review state transitions: verify the node correctly updates ENR fields when moving from 'fork planned' to 'no fork planned' state.
3. Test edge case: immediately after a fork completes, verify the node resets next_fork_version and next_fork_epoch to the no-fork values.
4. Verify that ENR updates are propagated to peers in a timely manner after fork state changes.
5. Check for race conditions where ENR might temporarily advertise stale fork information.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-52, Supporting Node: actor-beacon-node, Invariant: p2p:SG-012:INV-003

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for tb-009: Does the ENR eth2 field computation use wall-clock time instead of sync state for epoch determination?
checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Identify the code responsible for computing the ENR eth2 field fork_digest in the beacon node.
2. Trace the epoch value used in fork_digest calculation to determine its source (wall-clock vs sync state).
3. Set up a test node that is still syncing (behind chain head by multiple epochs).
4. Capture the ENR advertisement from the syncing node and extract the fork_digest field.
5. Compare the advertised fork_digest against what would be expected from: (a) wall-clock epoch, (b) sync-state epoch.
6. Verify that the fork_digest matches the wall-clock epoch, not the synced-to epoch.
7. Test across a simulated fork boundary where a syncing node would be behind the fork but wall-clock time is past the fork.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-53, Trust Boundary: tb-009, Invariant: p2p:SG-012:INV-004. A syncing node using sync-state epoch would advertise a pre-fork fork_digest after the fork has occurred, causing it to be filtered out by fully-synced peers.

checklist_item.title: Can a syncing beacon node become isolated from the P2P network by advertising an incorrect pre-fork fork_digest?
checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Deploy a test beacon node and allow it to fully sync to chain head.
2. Configure a second test node to start syncing from genesis (or significantly behind chain head).
3. Simulate a network fork transition while the second node is still syncing.
4. Monitor peer discovery attempts from the syncing node to fully-synced peers.
5. Verify whether the syncing node can successfully connect to post-fork peers.
6. Check ENR advertisements from the syncing node for fork_digest correctness.
7. Document any peer filtering or connection rejections that occur due to fork_digest mismatch.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-53, Node: actor-beacon-node. Bug bounty target: Demonstrate a syncing node being isolated because it advertises pre-fork fork_digest.

checklist_item.title: Does the beacon node correctly update its ENR eth2 field when wall-clock time crosses a fork epoch boundary?
checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Identify the ENR update mechanism and the trigger for eth2 field updates.
2. Set up a beacon node with a known fork schedule where a fork is imminent.
3. Monitor the node's ENR eth2 field continuously as wall-clock time approaches the fork epoch.
4. Verify that the ENR is updated precisely when wall-clock time crosses the fork boundary.
5. Confirm that the update occurs regardless of the node's sync state (even if still syncing pre-fork blocks).
6. Test edge cases: node restart near fork boundary, network partition during fork transition.
7. Validate that the sequence number in ENR is incremented appropriately on fork_digest change.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-53, Trust Boundary: tb-009. Fork transitions must be handled based on wall-clock time to prevent bootstrapping problems.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Can the slotted_freezable mechanism for Header objects be bypassed?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Attempt to modify Header fields after freeze() is called. 2. Test __dict__ manipulation on frozen Header objects. 3. Try subclassing Header to bypass freeze mechanism. 4. Verify that all modification attempts raise appropriate errors.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W7B16-17, Trust Boundary: tb-005. Internal-only reachability but high impact if bypassed.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Are all 21 header fields validated before block acceptance?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Craft blocks with zero/default values for computed fields (base_fee_per_gas, requests_hash, blob_gas_used). 2. Submit via P2P and verify rejection. 3. Test each of the 21 header fields individually with missing/default values. 4. Verify block validation detects and rejects incomplete headers.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W7B16-18, Trust Boundary: tb-005. Bug bounty eligible - PoC requires block with zero-valued computed field being accepted.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is base_fee_per_gas independently recomputed per EIP-1559?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Construct block with incorrect base_fee_per_gas (not matching EIP-1559 formula). 2. Submit via P2P. 3. Verify block is rejected. 4. Test boundary cases where parent gas_used is at limits. 5. Verify the adjustment formula is applied correctly.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W7B16-19, Trust Boundary: tb-005. Bug bounty eligible.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is blob gas accounting validated per EIP-4844 under Fusaka rules?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Construct block with incorrect blob_gas_used or excess_blob_gas. 2. Test with Fusaka-specific blob gas parameters. 3. Verify rejection of blocks with invalid blob gas values. 4. Test edge cases at the new Fusaka blob gas limits.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W7B16-20, Trust Boundary: tb-005. Fusaka-specific - higher risk of implementation inconsistencies.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is requests_hash validated per EIP-7685 under Fusaka?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Construct block with incorrect requests_hash. 2. Submit via P2P under Fusaka rules. 3. Verify block rejection. 4. Test EIP-7685 computation rules are correctly implemented. 5. Verify requests_hash is independently computed, not trusted from header.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W7B16-21, Trust Boundary: tb-005. Fusaka-specific EIP - high priority.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Are non-empty ommers rejected in post-merge blocks?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Construct post-merge Osaka block with non-empty ommers list. 2. Submit via P2P. 3. Verify body-level validation rejects the block. 4. Confirm both header (ommers_hash) and body (ommers tuple) validation occurs.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W7B16-23, Trust Boundary: tb-001. Data smuggling risk via ommer fields.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Are all 5 transaction types decoded without type confusion?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Construct blocks with each transaction type (Legacy, AccessList, FeeMarket, Blob, SetCode). 2. Test mixed blocks with all types. 3. Verify no type confusion between Bytes and LegacyTransaction representations. 4. Test edge cases in transaction type detection.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W7B16-24, Trust Boundary: tb-001. Bug bounty eligible.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is Withdrawal object immutability enforced after freeze()?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Create Withdrawal object and call freeze(). 2. Attempt to modify index, validator_index, address, and amount fields. 3. Verify all modification attempts raise errors. 4. Test __dict__ manipulation bypass attempts.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W7B16-25, Trust Boundary: tb-004. Internal reachability via Engine API.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Are incomplete Withdrawal objects rejected during construction?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Attempt to construct Withdrawal with missing fields. 2. Test each field individually (index, validator_index, address, amount). 3. Verify construction errors are raised for incomplete objects. 4. Test default value handling.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W7B16-26, Trust Boundary: tb-004.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Are withdrawal addresses and amounts type-validated?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify withdrawal address must be valid 20-byte Address. 2. Verify amount must be valid U256. 3. Test with invalid address lengths. 4. Test with amounts exceeding U256 bounds. 5. Confirm EL trusts but type-validates CL data.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W7B16-27, Trust Boundary: tb-004. CL trust assumption - EL does not verify withdrawal amounts.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is ETH credited only to addresses specified by CL?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Trace withdrawal processing code path. 2. Verify ETH is credited exactly to the address in each Withdrawal object. 3. Check for any address modification or substitution during processing. 4. Audit the withdrawal crediting function.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W7B16-28, Trust Boundary: tb-004. Requires CL compromise to exploit.

checklist_item.title: Is the bloom filter bytearray always exactly 256 bytes with no out-of-bounds access?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Review add_to_bloom function for bounds checking. 2. Craft transactions producing logs with adversarial addresses/topics. 3. Verify bloom bytearray is always 256 bytes. 4. Test for index values that could exceed [0, 255].
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W7B16-29. Bug bounty eligible - PoC must demonstrate out-of-bounds access.

checklist_item.title: Is the 11-bit mask (0x07FF) correctly applied in bloom bit computation?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify bit_to_set = Uint.from_be_bytes(hash[idx:idx+2]) AND 0x07FF. 2. Check for incorrect mask values (e.g., 0x0FFF). 3. Verify bit_to_set is always in [0, 2047]. 4. Test with hash values that would exceed bounds without proper masking.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W7B16-30. Incorrect mask causes consensus splits.

checklist_item.title: Is byte_index computation always in range [0, 255]?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify formula: bit_index = 0x07FF - bit_to_set, byte_index = bit_index // 8. 2. Test boundary cases where bit_to_set = 0 and bit_to_set = 2047. 3. Confirm byte_index always in [0, 255] given correct mask.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W7B16-31. Derived invariant from mask correctness.

checklist_item.title: Does add_to_bloom iterate exactly 3 times reading 2-byte pairs?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Review add_to_bloom loop structure. 2. Verify exactly 3 iterations reading indices (0, 2, 4). 3. Verify each iteration sets exactly one bit. 4. Check for off-by-one in loop bounds.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W7B16-32. Incorrect iteration count causes bloom divergence.

checklist_item.title: Is bloom filter initialized to 256 zero bytes before processing logs?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Review logs_bloom initialization code. 2. Check for buffer reuse without clearing. 3. Verify bloom is fresh 256 zero bytes before each computation. 4. Test for residual bits from previous computations.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W7B16-34. Memory optimization bugs could cause contamination.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is every log address added to bloom exactly once?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Trace bloom computation for receipts with multiple logs. 2. Verify each log address is processed exactly once. 3. Check for skipped addresses in edge cases. 4. Test with many logs in a single receipt.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W7B16-35, Trust Boundary: tb-008. Bug bounty eligible.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is every log topic added to bloom exactly once?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify add_to_bloom calls = (1 + len(log.topics)) per log. 2. Test logs with 0, 1, 2, 3, 4 topics. 3. Check for skipped topics. 4. Verify total bloom bit count matches expected.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W7B16-36, Trust Boundary: tb-008. Bug bounty eligible.
